---
title: "Welcome!"
---

<img src="/images/headshot.jpg" width="30%" hspace="20" align="center">

I am a PhD student in the Computational Story Lab in the Vermont Complex Systems Institute at the University of Vermont.

::: {.column-page-inset .text-center}
```{=html}
<a class="btn btn-outline-dark", href="mailto:calla.beauregard@uvm.edu" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">
    <i class="ai bi-envelope" role='img' aria-label='Email'></i> Email
</a>
<a class="btn btn-outline-dark", href="https://github.com/elizabyanne" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">
    <i class="bi bi-github" role='img' aria-label='Github'></i> Github
</a>
```
:::

## Research Interests
* Computational social sciences 
* Health and storytelling
* Evidenced-based social interventions
* Digital epidemiology
* Bayesian Machine Learning modeling in reproductive health 

## Personal Interests
* Mountains 
* Books
* Dogs (shout out to Senny!)
* Finn-Merino Sheep (ours are named Wallace and Gromit!)
* Volunteering as an social media/Google Non-Profit Ads coordinator with 
<a href="https://wood4good.org/">Wood4Good</a> to help end heating insecurity in Vermont!

## Recent News
* **November 2025**: 'Detecting sub-populations in online health communities: A mixed-methods 
exploration of breastfeeding messages in BabyCenter Birth Clubs' (accepted to <a href="https://sciforum.net/event/IOCSO2026">1st Annual International Online Conferences
on Societies 2026)</a> and published to <a href="https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2510.23692">
arXiv:2510.23692</a>

* **Fall 2025**: Led data analysis K-12 outreach events-- South Burlington High School research students, Girls Who Code
(Burlington Chapter), Women Can Do Conference (Randolph, VT)

* **September 2025**: Started consultancy, Spire Training Group

* **July 2025**: Fellowship, National Museum of Mathematics (MoMath) 
Mathematics Outreach Seminar and Training (MOST)

* **June 2025**: arXiv:<a href="https://arxiv.org/html/2506.21808v1">
2506.21808v1</a> [cs.CL] 'A suite of allotaxonometric tools for the comparison of complex systems 
using rank-turbulence divergence.' (co-author to St. Onge J, Fehr AMA, et al.)

* **June 2025**: JMIR Formative Research <a href="https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40554798/">article</a> published 
'Misrepresentation of Overall and By-Gender Mortality Causes in Film Using Online, Crowd-Sourced Data: Quantitative Analysis' (Beauregard, Danforth & Dodds, 2025)

* **April 2025**: arXiv <a href="http://arxiv.org/abs/2412.10924">pre-print</a> 'Tokens, the oft-overlooked appetizer: 
Large language models, the distributional hypothesis, and meaning' (Zimmerman et al., 2025)

* **April 2024**: 'Habitat Blocks: A Network Approach' poster recognized by Center for Research on Vermont Annual Meeting 2024
